title: buxus benguellensis var. hirta
intro: Buxus benguellensis var. hirta, also known as the hairy boxwood, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Buxus benguellensis plant, a type of boxwood native to Angola. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The hairy boxwood is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have several health benefits, including protecting against oxidative stress, improving immune function, and potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Buxus benguellensis var. hirta can be made into a tea or infused in oil to be used topically for its medicinal properties.
